jokes.text: Don't know. Don't care. But so far, you're all getting As.
jokes.setup: Student asks if her answer is correct on an anthropology exam
jokes.punchline: Professor admits he doesn't know or care but is giving everyone As anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - drunk professor's complete academic negligence delivered with casual indifference. Economy is excellent.

jokes.text: Throw paper balls at her head until she sits down.
jokes.setup: Student questions the fairness of everyone getting As
jokes.punchline: Professor casually suggests physical violence as classroom management
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny subversion of teacher authority. The casualness makes it work - perfect economy of language.

jokes.text: Student immediately complies and sits down after paper ball suggestion
jokes.setup: Duncan suggests throwing paper at the complaining student
jokes.punchline: Student immediately and fearfully complies with 'Sitting. I'm sitting.'
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the paper ball setup. The immediate compliance is funny but not exceptional.

jokes.text: It's Troy. / Hey, the man wants to give Darrell an A, let him do it.
jokes.setup: Duncan calls Troy 'Darrell' by mistake
jokes.punchline: Troy corrects him, but Abed says to let Duncan give 'Darrell' the A instead
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on the name mix-up. Abed's pragmatic response is very in character.

jokes.text: A toast to the easiest school, the dumbest dean... And the ultimate blow off class. Anthropology. Who knew?
jokes.setup: Students are celebrating their fake easy class
jokes.punchline: Jeff toasts to how pathetic their situation is
jokes.explanation: Classic Jeff cynicism. Good character beat but not particularly original or surprising.

jokes.text: Well, if it's my child, it's okay to have a drink or two. Chang babies love the sauce, you know? Alcohol and duck.
jokes.setup: Shirley explains she can't drink because she's pregnant
jokes.punchline: Chang suggests it's fine if it's his baby because Chang babies love alcohol and duck
jokes.explanation: Peak Chang absurdity - the specificity of 'alcohol and duck' is brilliantly weird. Perfect character integration.

jokes.text: Because I don't see any locusts.
jokes.setup: Chang asks how Shirley knows the baby isn't his
jokes.punchline: Shirley references biblical plagues, implying Chang would bring apocalyptic signs
jokes.explanation: Brilliant biblical reference from Christian Shirley. The implication that Chang would herald the apocalypse is perfectly crafted.

jokes.text: You have a booger. / I know. It's part of me.
jokes.setup: Britta talks about women's connectedness to their bodies
jokes.punchline: When told she has a booger, she calmly accepts it as 'part of me'
jokes.explanation: Perfect subversion of Britta's pretentious moment about body awareness. The deadpan delivery makes it work.

jokes.text: Question number two: How awesome is it that this class is fake?
jokes.setup: Duncan is administering what should be a serious anthropology exam
jokes.punchline: The exam question directly acknowledges the class is fake
jokes.explanation: Good meta humor typical of Community. The brazenness of putting it on the exam is funny.

jokes.text: And that is what Jews do at weddings.
jokes.setup: Duncan appears to be explaining some anthropological concept
jokes.punchline: He's apparently been describing Jewish wedding customs in a completely inappropriate context
jokes.explanation: Moderately funny non-sequitur but lacks context to be really effective. Feels somewhat random.

jokes.text: You know, like that film Children of the Corn.
jokes.setup: Duncan describes himself as a father figure to students due to his educational approach
jokes.punchline: He compares himself to a horror movie about murderous children
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - starts wholesome, ends with horror reference. Shows Duncan's complete lack of self-awareness.

jokes.text: Do you know what, I have left the exam paper in my office. But it is a real doozy. There is an interactive element. A performance component. And I don't want to tip it, but there may be a lump of clay coming to everyone in the room.
jokes.setup: Duncan realizes he forgot the exam but needs to save face in front of the reporter
jokes.punchline: He improvises increasingly ridiculous exam components including mysterious clay
jokes.explanation: Good escalating improvisation showing Duncan's desperation. The clay element is appropriately random.

jokes.text: Wowee. This is a real college.
jokes.setup: Reporter has just witnessed Duncan's completely unprofessional behavior
jokes.punchline: He sarcastically marvels at the 'real college' he's observing
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan reaction to the chaos. Simple but effective sarcasm with good economy.

jokes.text: You know what, not different. Equal. Equal to whites. You know what, better than whites.
jokes.setup: Troy starts to describe different nationalities of food trucks
jokes.punchline: He awkwardly over-corrects his language about racial equality in an increasingly uncomfortable way
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Troy's well-meaning but clumsy attempts at political correctness. Good escalation structure.

jokes.text: Jordan was in here so long, he came out with a driver's license. I'm kidding. That's not true.
jokes.setup: Shirley explains her previous babies were late
jokes.punchline: Exaggerates that one was so late he had a driver's license, then immediately clarifies it's a joke
jokes.explanation: Classic Shirley - makes a joke then immediately clarifies she's joking. The self-correction is very in character.

jokes.text: Chang babies are always premature. We gestate fast because we're better at obtaining nutrients. Some need only eight months, depending on how much of the mother they eat.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Shirley's baby timing
jokes.punchline: Chang describes Chang babies as parasitic creatures that eat their mothers
jokes.explanation: Peak Chang absurdity. The matter-of-fact delivery of increasingly horrifying 'facts' about Chang babies is brilliant. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Yeah, let me guess who paid for that study. Big antacid.
jokes.setup: Shirley mentions her doctor said antacid is safe
jokes.punchline: Britta invokes a conspiracy theory about 'Big antacid'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Britta moment - applying Big Pharma conspiracy thinking to antacids. Great economy and character integration.

jokes.text: Give you $1,000. / Indecent Proposal. Indecent Proposal.
jokes.setup: Pierce wants to buy Troy and Abed's handshake for $1000
jokes.punchline: Troy and Abed immediately reference the movie Indecent Proposal about selling something precious
jokes.explanation: Good reference humor that works because it's exactly what Troy and Abed would do. Economy is good.

jokes.text: No. / I don't know.
jokes.setup: Someone asks if Troy and Abed are going to fall in love
jokes.punchline: Troy says no definitively, Abed is uncertain, creating awkward tension
jokes.explanation: Good character beat playing with the Troy/Abed dynamic. The different responses create perfect awkwardness.

jokes.text: An epidural is a proper Christian woman's only chance to get wrecked.
jokes.setup: Discussion about natural vs medicated childbirth
jokes.punchline: Shirley frames medical pain relief as her only acceptable way to get high
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Shirley line combining her Christianity with unexpected drug humor. Perfect economy and character integration.

jokes.text: That's not something a woman does on her child's head.
jokes.setup: Britta suggests natural birth might give women orgasms
jokes.punchline: Shirley is mortified at the inappropriate suggestion involving her child
jokes.explanation: Perfect Shirley reaction to inappropriate sexual content. Her moral outrage is both funny and in character.

jokes.text: First of all, together, my cats can do anything.
jokes.setup: Jeff insults Britta's two one-eyed cats
jokes.punchline: Britta defends them by claiming they're more capable together
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist Britta logic. The earnest defense of her disabled cats is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Oh, and you're gonna squat in a babbling brook and a beautiful star child is going to slide out on a ray of sunshine while your cats each half watch because you're so natural.
jokes.setup: Jeff mocking Britta's natural birth philosophy
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous imagery ending with the detail about her one-eyed cats 'half watching'
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating mockery with great callback to the cats. The 'half watch' detail is brilliantly specific.

jokes.text: I don't want my baby's first memory to be star-burns.
jokes.setup: Shirley realizes she might give birth in the classroom
jokes.punchline: She's horrified that Star-Burns would be her baby's first sight
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Shirley's maternal concern mixed with judgment of Star-Burns. Economical and in character.

jokes.text: I drive a Prius, which I think is pretty cool.
jokes.setup: Dean is offering to drive Shirley to the hospital
jokes.punchline: He awkwardly tries to make his car choice sound cool during a medical emergency
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dean moment - completely inappropriate timing for his insecurity about being cool. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: He's 'Sugar Boots' in my phone. / That hurts, Shirley.
jokes.setup: Shirley asks someone to call Andre
jokes.punchline: Chang is hurt that Andre has a cute nickname while presumably he doesn't
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Chang's jealousy and need for affection. The timing during the emergency adds to the absurdity.

jokes.text: We came so close to having one class that wasn't all about them.
jokes.setup: The study group's drama is once again taking over
jokes.punchline: Annie laments that they can never have a normal class experience
jokes.explanation: Mild meta humor about the show's structure. Good Annie frustration but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: This has got to be a Chang baby. Changs are never born in hospitals. More like taxicabs, hardware stores, bank lines, sewers. My nephew Jin was born on a treadmill at bally total fitness. 20% incline.
jokes.setup: Chang claims this must be his baby because of the circumstances
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous list of Chang birth locations, ending with specific treadmill details
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Chang mythology building. The escalation to '20% incline' is perfectly specific and absurd. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: You ever tried googling me? Can't be done!
jokes.setup: Chang explains why there are so many Changs
jokes.punchline: He claims there are so many Changs that he's ungoogleable
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist logic from Chang. The practical problem of being ungoogleable is funny and well-delivered.

jokes.text: According to my watch, it's 1,000 o'clock.
jokes.setup: Pierce is trying to buy the handshake for $1000
jokes.punchline: He creates a nonsensical time reference incorporating the money amount
jokes.explanation: Mildly clever Pierce wordplay. Not brilliant but has good economy and fits his character's weird humor.

jokes.text: All right. Betty Grable.
jokes.setup: Pierce has bought the rights to the handshake and wants to try it
jokes.punchline: He says 'Betty Grable' expecting them to do the handshake, but it doesn't work
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the handshake failure. Pierce's outdated reference is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Make your money, whore.
jokes.setup: Pierce is frustrated the handshake isn't working
jokes.punchline: He resorts to calling Troy a prostitute to force him to perform
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny escalation showing Pierce's entitled frustration. Great economy and perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Who's Betty Grable? / Yeah, that's the stuff.
jokes.setup: Troy asks who Betty Grable is
jokes.punchline: Pierce is satisfied by the question, showing he just wanted any reaction
jokes.explanation: Decent payoff showing Pierce just wanted engagement. The generational gap humor works but isn't exceptional.

jokes.text: It's like a million bucks in dog dollars.
jokes.setup: Pierce is enjoying the successful handshake transaction
jokes.punchline: He makes a nonsensical comparison to 'dog dollars'
jokes.explanation: Mild Pierce absurdity. 'Dog dollars' is appropriately nonsensical but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: It's like buying candy from a baby. / What did you do? You ruined it!
jokes.setup: Pierce brags about how easy it was to buy the handshake
jokes.punchline: Troy realizes Pierce has destroyed something pure, getting genuinely upset
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Troy's innocence vs Pierce's cynicism. The emotional turn works well.

jokes.text: Once you've sold something out, you can't get it back.
jokes.setup: Discussion about the ruined handshake
jokes.punchline: Abed makes a broader point about commercialization destroying authenticity
jokes.explanation: Typical Abed wisdom delivered matter-of-factly. Good thematic statement but not particularly funny.

jokes.text: You two are dumber than Jackie Coogan. / He divorced Betty Grable.
jokes.setup: Pierce insults Troy and Abed with an obscure reference
jokes.punchline: He explains the reference, showing his obsession with old Hollywood
jokes.explanation: Good callback and character consistency. Pierce's outdated references are in character but not hilarious.

jokes.text: Pierce tainted our special handshake with his blood money and now we can't get the magic back!
jokes.setup: Troy is upset about selling the handshake
jokes.punchline: He dramatically describes Pierce's money as 'blood money' and mourns the lost magic
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Troy's emotional investment. The 'blood money' escalation is appropriately dramatic.

jokes.text: Holy crap. You two for real? / Nobody cares about your stupid handshake. A baby is coming out of Shirley's vagina two feet from us.
jokes.setup: Troy and Abed are seriously upset about their handshake during a birth
jokes.punchline: Jeff points out how ridiculous their priorities are given the circumstances
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff reaction - cutting through the absurdity with brutal honesty. Good structure and economy.

jokes.text: I delivered a baby earlier this year in the back of an SUV. / Where was I when that happened? / I don't know. Off in the background.
jokes.setup: Abed reveals he's delivered a baby before
jokes.punchline: Jeff asks where he was, Abed says 'off in the background' - referencing how Jeff isn't always central to every story
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta humor about TV show structure. Abed breaking the fourth wall about Jeff being 'in the background' is perfectly crafted.

jokes.text: I guess some national ethnicities don't play nice with others and things have turned into a bit of a race... Kerfuffle.
jokes.setup: Dean is trying to diplomatically explain the food festival disaster
jokes.punchline: He almost says 'race riot' but catches himself and says 'kerfuffle'
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing the Dean's political correctness. The pause before 'kerfuffle' sells it well.

jokes.text: I didn't even know there was a difference between North and South Korean barbeque. I mean, M*A*S*H lasted longer than that war. Get over it. Am I right? Don't print that.
jokes.setup: Dean is explaining the ethnic food fight
jokes.punchline: He makes increasingly inappropriate comments about the Korean War, then realizes he's on record
jokes.explanation: Great escalating discomfort as the Dean digs himself deeper. The 'don't print that' ending is perfect.

jokes.text: Shirley, pretend I'm saying this in a soothing, reassuring, non-robotic voice. We're not going to make it to the hospital.
jokes.setup: Abed needs to deliver bad news to Shirley
jokes.punchline: He asks her to imagine he's saying it with emotion since he can't actually do that
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Abed moment - pure meta-comedy about his own character limitations. Perfect self-awareness and integration.

jokes.text: In which case, what you need to do now is elevate your legs over your head while facing the northeast corner of the room. But try not to look at the door, okay? That's bad luck and Chang babies are very superstitious. Also if you can visualize a rabbit riding a dragon, it'll increase his chances of winning lotteries.
jokes.setup: Chang gives birth advice if the baby is his
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous and specific superstitious instructions ending with lottery-winning rabbits
jokes.explanation: Peak Chang absurdity - the escalation from birth position to rabbit lottery visualization is brilliantly specific and insane.

jokes.text: I can do it! I can do it! I'm good at looking!
jokes.setup: Shirley needs someone to check her cervix dilation
jokes.punchline: Britta volunteers with childlike enthusiasm, citing 'looking' as her qualification
jokes.explanation: Perfect Britta moment - inappropriate enthusiasm with terrible reasoning. 'I'm good at looking' is brilliantly stupid.

jokes.text: No problowe, Rob Lowe.
jokes.setup: Britta is trying to sound confident about helping with the birth
jokes.punchline: She makes a nonsensical rhyme with Rob Lowe
jokes.explanation: Typical Britta forced wordplay. Mildly amusing but not particularly clever - feels random.

jokes.text: Britta's horrified reaction after looking
jokes.setup: Britta confidently volunteers to check Shirley's dilation
jokes.punchline: Her shocked expression shows she wasn't prepared for what she saw
jokes.explanation: Good visual payoff to her overconfidence. The reaction beat effectively shows her horror without being explicit.

jokes.text: I take it the head has reached the cervix.
jokes.setup: Britta has a horrified reaction after looking
jokes.punchline: Abed calmly deduces the situation from her expression
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed deadpan reading of the situation. Great economy and character consistency.

jokes.text: Well, a lot more are conceived. / No, Paul, that's off the record and not true. Sort of.
jokes.setup: Reporter asks if many babies are born in classrooms
jokes.punchline: Duncan implies more are conceived there, then awkwardly backtracks while admitting it's sort of true
jokes.explanation: Good Duncan inappropriate comment with realistic backtracking. The 'sort of' admission makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I need to take a class, or read a book, or shadow a nurse, or at least have my mom hug me first.
jokes.setup: Annie is panicking about delivering a baby
jokes.punchline: Her list of needed qualifications escalates to needing maternal comfort
jokes.explanation: Good Annie panic with nice escalation ending on the mom hug. Very in character for her neurotic overpreparation.

jokes.text: Seven generations of little scrappers born in the worst conditions you can imagine... My cousin slid out feet first in a pickle barrel. My uncle was born during rainy season in a Mongolian sheep pasture. My brother, hmm, born under a crowded noodle bar while my grandfather finished his happy dancing shrimp.
jokes.setup: Chang is telling stories to calm Shirley about Chang baby resilience
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific and ridiculous birth stories ending with the grandfather prioritizing his meal
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant Chang mythology. Each birth story is more absurd, ending with the perfect detail about happy dancing shrimp. Peak Community writing.

jokes.text: Look, Britta, there is something in you that wants to take care of people so bad that you'll do it until you puke. That's what Shirley needs right now.
jokes.setup: Jeff is trying to convince Britta to help with the birth
jokes.punchline: He describes her caring nature as obsessive to the point of vomiting
jokes.explanation: Good Jeff insight into Britta's character. 'Until you puke' is a perfect specific detail about her over-caring.

jokes.text: Are you saying I'll be a good mom? / What? No. Man, you will really force anything, won't you?
jokes.setup: Britta tries to interpret Jeff's pep talk as about motherhood
jokes.punchline: Jeff is appalled at her self-serving interpretation during an emergency
jokes.explanation: Perfect character interaction - Britta's narcissism vs Jeff's exasperation. 'Force anything' is perfectly crafted.

jokes.text: Well, don't tell any doctors I said this, but at this point in time, the bus pretty much drives itself.
jokes.setup: Annie asks if childbirth delivery is really just telling Shirley to push
jokes.punchline: Abed uses a bus metaphor to explain that birth is mostly automatic
jokes.explanation: Good Abed practical wisdom with nice metaphor. The 'don't tell doctors' disclaimer adds authenticity.

jokes.text: Who exactly is the bus in this scenario?
jokes.setup: Abed says the bus drives itself regarding childbirth
jokes.punchline: Annie wants clarification on the metaphor's uncomfortable implications
jokes.explanation: Good callback questioning the metaphor's logic. Annie's literal-mindedness is in character.

jokes.text: Hundreds of Changs were born underneath the earth. Most spent their first five years underground.
jokes.setup: Chang continues his Chang baby resilience stories
jokes.punchline: Implies Chang children living underground like mole people for years
jokes.explanation: Great continuation of Chang mythology. Underground Chang children is perfectly absurd and dark.

jokes.text: Guys, I got it. I'm in the zone.
jokes.setup: Medical professionals arrive and offer to take over the delivery
jokes.punchline: Britta refuses help because she's found her calling in this one moment
jokes.explanation: Good Britta character moment - her brief competence going to her head. Very believable reaction.

jokes.text: And it's also a black person! Not that that's important.
jokes.setup: Britta announces the baby's birth
jokes.punchline: She unnecessarily points out the race then awkwardly tries to downplay it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Britta racial awkwardness. Her attempt to be progressive while being inappropriate is spot-on.

jokes.text: I never told you this part, but all Changs are born with tails. You kind of dodged a bullet there.
jokes.setup: The baby is confirmed to not be Chang's
jokes.punchline: Chang reveals Changs supposedly have tails, making this sound like good news
jokes.explanation: Brilliant final Chang baby reveal. Tails are perfectly absurd, and 'dodged a bullet' makes it even funnier.

jokes.text: It's back. / It never left.
jokes.setup: Troy and Abed seem to reconcile after the handshake drama
jokes.punchline: They do their handshake again, suggesting their bond survived commercialization
jokes.explanation: Nice resolution to the handshake plot. Simple but effective with good economy.

jokes.text: After a fierce campaign, I happily concede this baby to you. Pretty classy of me.
jokes.setup: Chang formally acknowledges the baby isn't his
jokes.punchline: He frames it as a political concession and congratulates himself for his grace
jokes.explanation: Good Chang self-aggrandizement. Treating baby paternity like an election is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Ben Bennett? / Oh, damn. / Okay. No, no. No, that's fine. Ben Bennett. Hi, Ben Bennett.
jokes.setup: Shirley wants to name the baby Ben after Chang
jokes.punchline: The alliteration of 'Ben Bennett' sounds ridiculous, and Chang's disappointment is obvious
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay joke. The alliteration sounds silly and Chang's reaction adds to it.

jokes.text: Oh, look, he said Chang. / No. / No, he didn't.
jokes.setup: Baby makes baby noises
jokes.punchline: Chang desperately tries to claim the baby said his name but everyone shuts it down
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Chang's desperation for connection. The immediate shutdown is funny.

jokes.text: Can you believe that's how we come into this world? Screaming, crying, covered in crap? And then somewhere along the lines, we get it into our heads that we're destined for greatness.
jokes.setup: Jeff reflects on the birth they just witnessed
jokes.punchline: He contrasts our humble beginnings with our grand self-perceptions
jokes.explanation: Standard Jeff philosophizing. Competent but not particularly original or funny.

jokes.text: We are. I just yanked a little dude out of my friend.
jokes.setup: Jeff questions whether we're destined for greatness
jokes.punchline: Britta affirms we are, citing her delivery as evidence of her own greatness
jokes.explanation: Good Britta response - turning Jeff's philosophy into personal validation. In character but not exceptional.

jokes.text: His fingers are like good & plenties. I know. I want to eat them. Which is lame. Totally.
jokes.setup: They're admiring the baby's tiny features
jokes.punchline: Troy compares fingers to candy and admits wanting to eat them, Abed agrees it's lame
jokes.explanation: Mild Troy and Abed moment. The candy comparison is okay but their self-awareness about being lame saves it.

jokes.text: I am a good dean! Whoo!
jokes.setup: Reporter suggests the dean will get positive coverage
jokes.punchline: Dean celebrates his success with childlike enthusiasm and a victory dance
jokes.explanation: Classic Dean celebration. His desperate need for validation is in character but not particularly inventive.

jokes.text: Cancel school today or we tell everybody about this.
jokes.setup: Troy and Abed discover Duncan's fake anthropology class
jokes.punchline: They blackmail him to cancel school, showing they've learned to work the system
jokes.explanation: Nice bookend callback. Shows character growth in learning to manipulate the system they've been part of.
